raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The state has failed to systemically track homelessness spending.",
    "The state is not adequately positioned to collect data and assess outcomes related to homelessness programs.",
    "Without changes to increase transparency and reporting, policymakers will not be able to properly assess the effectiveness of homelessness programs.",
    "Only two of the five state programs could provide enough information to assess their cost effectiveness.",
    "Neither San Jose nor San Diego centrally tracks and reports its spending and outcomes related to homelessness.",
    "Neither San Jose nor San Diego has evaluated the effects of its homelessness programs.",
    "Both San Jose and San Diego have sent funding to outside service providers without assessing whether these service providers are getting results.",
    "California has spent over $20 billion taxpayer dollars to solve the homelessness crisis since 2018.",
    "The overall homeless population in California has increased by 32 percent since 2018.",
    "Half of the nation’s unsheltered homeless now live in California.",
    "The homeless population in Sacramento County has grown by 67% since 2019."
  ]
}
```
